# tzf

```go
import "github.com/ringsaturn/tzf"
```

Package tzf is a package convert \(lng,lat\) to timezone.

Inspired by timezonefinder https://github.com/jannikmi/timezonefinder, fast python package for finding the timezone of any point on earth \(coordinates\) offline.

## type [DefaultFinder](<https://github.com/ringsaturn/tzf/blob/main/tzf_default_finder.go#L15-L18>)

DefaultFinder is a finder impl combine both [FuzzyFinder](<#FuzzyFinder>) and [Finder](<#Finder>).

It's designed for performance first and allow some not so correct return at some area.

<a name="DefaultFinder.GetTimezoneName"></a>

### func \(\*DefaultFinder\) [GetTimezoneName](<https://github.com/ringsaturn/tzf/blob/main/tzf_default_finder.go#L61>)

```go
func (f *DefaultFinder) GetTimezoneName(lng float64, lat float64) string
```

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
 "fmt"

 "github.com/ringsaturn/tzf"
)

func main() {
 finder, err := tzf.NewDefaultFinder()
 if err != nil {
  panic(err)
 }
 fmt.Println(finder.GetTimezoneName(116.6386, 40.0786)) // In longitude-latitude order
}
```

#### Output

```
Asia/Shanghai
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Finder"></a>

## type [Finder](<https://github.com/ringsaturn/tzf/blob/main/tzf.go#L90-L97>)

Finder is based on point\-in\-polygon search algo.

Memory will use about 100MB if lite data and 1G if full data. Performance is very stable and very accuate.

```go

    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

```go
func (f *Finder) GetTimezoneName(lng float64, lat float64) string
```

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
 "fmt"

 "github.com/ringsaturn/tzf"

 tzfrellite "github.com/ringsaturn/tzf-rel-lite"

 pb "github.com/ringsaturn/tzf/gen/go/tzf/v1"
 "google.golang.org/protobuf/proto"
)

func main() {
 input := &pb.Timezones{}
 if err := proto.Unmarshal(tzfrellite.LiteData, input); err != nil {
  panic(err)
 }
 finder, _ := tzf.NewFinderFromPB(input)
 fmt.Println(finder.GetTimezoneName(116.6386, 40.0786))
}
```

#### Output

```
Asia/Shanghai
```

</p>
</details>

# preindex

```go
import "github.com/ringsaturn/tzf/preindex"
```

Package preindex

### Background

The Ray Casting algorithm's time complexity is $O\(n^2\)$ which is expensive for high throughput API that severing geo based data like weather forecasts. And most of these requests are came from big cities around the world.

If we can reduce these location's query execution times, our API could got performance improvements.

### How to

Preindex's logic is very simple, generate map tiles around a multi polygon, and exclude 1/2 edge layer, then merge to upper tiles. Then dumps all the tiles's X/Y/Z and timezone to Protocol Buffer based data.

A sample image of output tiles show on maps: https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/13536789/200174943-7d40661e-bda5-4b79-a867-ec637e245a49.png

Some areas are excluded from prefindex, since default most small tile \`level==11\` couldn't cover polygon shape details, see [tzf\\\#76](<https://github.com/ringsaturn/tzf/issues/76>) for more backgrounds. Latest excluded areas can be views here [exclude.geojson](<http://geojson.io/#data=data:text/x-url,https%3A%2F%2Fraw.githubusercontent.com%2Fringsaturn%2Ftzf%2Fmain%2Fpreindex%2Fexclude.geojson>)

<a name="Polygon"></a>

## type [Polygon](<https://github.com/ringsaturn/tzf/blob/main/gen/go/tzf/v1/tzinfo.pb.go#L177-L183>)

Define a polygon, mostly based on GeoJSON's Polygon define.

Excerpt from RFC\-9476 section 'Polygon'

- A linear ring is a closed LineString with four or more positions.

- The first and last positions are equivalent, and they MUST contain identical values; their representation SHOULD also be identical.

- A linear ring is the boundary of a surface or the boundary of a hole in a surface.

- A linear ring MUST follow the right\-hand rule with respect to the area it bounds, i.e., exterior rings are counterclockwise, and holes are clockwise.
  
  Note: the [GJ2008](<https://geojson.org/geojson-spec>) specification did not discuss linear ring winding order. For backwards compatibility, parsers SHOULD NOT reject Polygons that do not follow the right\-hand rule.
  
  Though a linear ring is not explicitly represented as a GeoJSON geometry type, it leads to a canonical formulation of the Polygon geometry type definition as follows:

- For type "Polygon", the "coordinates" member MUST be an array of linear ring coordinate arrays.

- For Polygons with more than one of these rings, the first MUST be the exterior ring, and any others MUST be interior rings. The exterior ring bounds the surface, and the interior rings \(if present\) bound holes within the surface.

```go
type Polygon struct {
    Points []*Point   `protobuf:"bytes,1,rep,name=points,proto3" json:"points,omitempty"` // define the "exterior ring"
    Holes  []*Polygon `protobuf:"bytes,2,rep,name=holes,proto3" json:"holes,omitempty"`   // define the "interior rings" as holes
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```
